policy.sourceRule: Gaps are derived from active-mission.md and from operational observations.
policy.linkRule: When a Discovery candidate addresses a known gap, tag the candidate with the gap_id.
policy.reviewRule: Review this registry whenever active-mission.md is updated.

gaps.description: Canonical Directive engine surface is only partially materialized as one reusable executable core with clear lane boundaries and host-adapter seams
gaps.current_state: Engine owns canonical state resolution (dw-state.ts), case planning from mirrored snapshots, negative-path validation across all architecture stages, structured checker output with failure contracts, a meta-checker (checker-definition-pilot), canonical-read-surface coverage enforcement, route-supersession logic, and case event tracking. Remaining sub-gaps decomposed into gap-engine-adaptation-logic, gap-engine-host-adapter-contract, and gap-engine-lane-boundary-enforcement.
gaps.desired_state: Engine owns substantially more intake, routing, adaptation/improvement, proof, decision, and handoff state while Discovery, Runtime, and Architecture operate as clear Engine lanes
gaps.resolution_notes: Substantially achieved: Engine now owns state resolution, case planning, negative-path validation, structured checker output, meta-checker, canonical-read-surface coverage enforcement, route-supersession logic, and case event tracking. Remaining work decomposed into three specific sub-gaps: gap-engine-adaptation-logic, gap-engine-host-adapter-contract, gap-engine-lane-boundary-enforcement.

gaps.description: Extract/Adapt/Improve workflow steps have no engine-owned logic; adaptation decisions are unstructured
gaps.current_state: Engine owns buildExtractionPlan, buildAdaptationPlan, and buildImprovementPlan in engine/directive-engine.ts with real lane-specific logic. Extraction feeds adaptation which feeds improvement (structured chaining). Events value_extracted, value_adapted, value_improved are tracked in run records. All three are exercised on every processSource call.
gaps.desired_state: Engine owns adaptation planning with structured extraction, adaptation, and improvement steps that can be tracked, evaluated, and reported through the canonical workflow
gaps.resolution_notes: Already built: buildExtractionPlan (line 380), buildAdaptationPlan (line 456), buildImprovementPlan (line 542) in engine/directive-engine.ts. These are private functions chained in processSource with structured stage input/output. The gap description was written before the existing logic was discovered during investigation.

gaps.description: No formal host-adapter boundary contract exists; hosts import engine and shared/lib internals freely
gaps.current_state: npm run check:host-adapter-boundary declares a 35-prefix adapter surface and validates all 26 host TS files across 4 host directories (integration-kit, standalone-host, web-host, workbench) only import from the declared surface. Any new host import from engine/ or shared/ must be added to the ALLOWED_HOST_IMPORT_PREFIXES list, making the boundary explicit and auditable.
gaps.desired_state: Explicit adapter surface that hosts must use, with at least one machine-checkable proof that host imports stay within the adapter boundary
gaps.resolution_notes: Created scripts/check-host-adapter-boundary.ts — a host adapter boundary checker that declares the full adapter surface as an explicit allowlist of 35 import prefixes and validates all 26 host TS files only import from the declared surface. Replaces the rejected dependency-cruiser approach with a targeted allowlist check.

gaps.description: Lane boundaries between Discovery, Runtime, and Architecture are doctrine-documented but not programmatically enforced
gaps.current_state: npm run check:lane-boundary-imports enforces directional import rules: engine must not import from hosts, shared must not import from hosts. Scans 12 engine files and 81 shared files. The shared-to-engine direction is allowed by design (canonical read surface composes engine truth). Discovery, Architecture directories have 0 TS files so no cross-lane import is possible there.
gaps.desired_state: At least one machine-checkable proof that lane-local logic stays lane-local and shared logic stays in Engine
gaps.resolution_notes: Created scripts/check-lane-boundary-imports.ts — a lightweight directional import checker that validates engine/ does not import from hosts/ and shared/lib/ does not import from hosts/. Scans all 93 product TS files with zero violations. Replaces the rejected dependency-cruiser and ESLint approaches with a targeted zero-dependency check.

gaps.description: Case modeling surface covers only 6 of 61 queue entries; case planner covers only 8; no unified case catalog exists
gaps.current_state: All 62 queue entries have case model files in state/cases/ with event logs in state/case-events/. Case planner covers 62/62 entries: 37 stop, 14 parked, 6 recommend_task, 5 waiting_review, 0 blocked. 40 entries have canonical resolution with currentStage and nextLegalStep. npm run report:case-catalog provides the unified catalog surface.
gaps.desired_state: Every active queue entry has a corresponding case model accessible through a unified catalog surface, and the case planner can recommend next steps for all non-terminal cases
gaps.resolution_notes: Backfilled 55 case models via scripts/backfill-case-models.ts. 33 with canonical resolution (state_materialized events), 22 minimal (legacy pre-routing). Added two planner rules: null-stage legacy entries map to stop, architecture.bounded_result.adopt maps to recommend_task(confirm_retention). Coverage went from 6/62 case files and 8 plannable to 62/62 case files and 62/62 plannable.

gaps.description: 39 engine runs exist as individual records with no cross-run trend analysis or performance pattern detection
gaps.current_state: npm run report:run-evidence-aggregation produces structured cross-run aggregation: lane distribution, usefulness distribution, source type diversity, confidence distribution, decision outcome patterns, integration mode patterns, temporal trend buckets, case lifecycle durations, and mission priority score stats.
gaps.desired_state: A report or check surface that aggregates run evidence over time: routing decision patterns, outcome distributions, case lifecycle durations, and adaptation quality indicators
gaps.resolution_notes: Created the canonical run-evidence aggregation surface (now at engine/execution/run-evidence-aggregation.ts) with aggregateRunEvidence plus scripts/report-run-evidence-aggregation.ts. Aggregates all 39 runs across 7 dimensions plus temporal trends and lifecycle summary.

gaps.description: Runtime chain complete through promotion-readiness but no host-facing promotion mechanism exists; cases are permanently parked at the boundary
gaps.current_state: npm run generate:promotion-specifications produces 6 structured JSON promotion specification artifacts at runtime/06-promotion-specifications/. Each spec fills promotion contract fields (integrationMode, targetRuntimeSurface, owner, sourceIntentArtifact, requiredGates, rollbackPlan, proofArtifactPath, proposedHost) from the promotion-readiness chain and explicitly lists openDecisions. The Scientify case has a concrete host (standalone-host), integration mode (reimplement), and implementation slices. npm run check:runtime-promotion-specification separately validates contract completeness.
gaps.desired_state: At least one bounded promotion pathway from promotion-readiness to a host-consumable artifact, even if non-executing
gaps.resolution_notes: Created scripts/generate-promotion-specifications.ts which produces actual host-consumable JSON promotion specifications at runtime/06-promotion-specifications/. Each file fills promotion contract fields from the promotion-readiness artifact chain, identifies open decisions, and describes what the host would receive. This is the bounded forward pathway from promotion-readiness to a host-consumable artifact — non-executing but structurally complete.

gaps.description: The first manual Scientify Runtime promotion cycle is explicit and checked, but Runtime promotion is still singleton evidence rather than a repeatable bounded operating path
gaps.current_state: Manual Runtime promotion is now proved across two bounded non-executing cases: the Scientify standalone-host promotion record and the OpenMOSS Directive Workspace web-host promotion record.
gaps.desired_state: At least one additional Runtime candidate advances through the same bounded manual promotion chain so Runtime promotion can be treated as repeatable operating truth instead of a one-off Scientify-only exception.
gaps.resolution_notes: Repeatability is now real: the second manual OpenMOSS DW web-host promotion record advances a different Runtime candidate through the same bounded non-executing promotion path, so Runtime promotion is no longer singleton Scientify-only evidence.

gaps.description: Discovery has no machine-readable intake queue for programmatic query or worker-pull
gaps.current_state: Markdown fast-path records only
gaps.desired_state: Shadow intake queue alongside markdown with schema validation
gaps.resolution_notes: Shadow intake queue created at discovery/intake-queue.json

gaps.description: Mission-conditioned usefulness is not operationalized as a machine-readable input
gaps.current_state: Usefulness evaluated by human judgment only
gaps.desired_state: Active-mission artifact referenced in Discovery templates
gaps.resolution_notes: Active-mission artifact created at knowledge/active-mission.md; fast-path template updated with mission-alignment fields

gaps.description: Behavior-preserving transformation has zero operational materialization despite being doctrine first-class
gaps.current_state: No templates, schemas, evaluators, or promotion profiles for transformation work
gaps.desired_state: Transformation record template, proof schema, evaluator contract, and promotion profile
gaps.resolution_notes: Transformation record template, proof schema, evaluator contract, and promotion profile created

gaps.description: Verifier lanes are gate-shaped (binary pass/fail) but not evaluator-shaped (dimensional measurement)
gaps.current_state: npm run check:* scripts provide pass/fail only
gaps.desired_state: Evaluator contract defining metric, baseline, measurement method, threshold, comparison mode
gaps.resolution_notes: Evaluator contract created at shared/contracts/evaluator-contract.md

gaps.description: OpenClaw-to-Discovery coordination is implicit, not contractual
gaps.current_state: OpenClaw-root helpers can append pending candidates into the primary Discovery queue through direct submission, stale verification freshness signaling, and maintenance/watchdog degraded-state signaling. The first two bounded paths are exercised; the third is implemented and checker-backed.
gaps.desired_state: Formal contract plus exercised bounded submission paths from OpenClaw root into Discovery intake.
gaps.resolution_notes: Root helpers at scripts/submit-openclaw-discovery-candidate.ps1, scripts/submit-openclaw-runtime-verification-signal.ps1, and scripts/submit-openclaw-maintenance-watchdog-signal.ps1 are active alongside the repo-native adapter checks in scripts/openclaw/. Candidates dw-openclaw-discovery-submission-flow and dw-openclaw-runtime-verification-freshness-2026-03-22 exercised the first two paths; dw-openclaw-maintenance-watchdog-signal-lane materialized the third lane without fabricating a live incident.

gaps.description: Monitor/defer review cadence has no automated enforcement
gaps.current_state: Monitor records have review dates but no checker flags overdue items
gaps.desired_state: Checker script scanning monitor records and flagging overdue reviews
gaps.resolution_notes: Resolved historically through a Discovery monitor-review cadence checker during the earlier external-host era. This gap remains closed, but the retired Mission Control script path is no longer the active repo-native operator surface.

gaps.description: Discovery intake queue has only 1 entry vs 53 routing records; shadow mode cannot prove itself toward 10-candidate transition threshold
gaps.current_state: Shadow queue with 1 completed entry; transition requires 10 candidates
gaps.desired_state: At least 10 candidates processed through both markdown and queue
gaps.resolution_notes: Backfilled 9 recent routing records into queue (total 10 entries, all valid). Queue has reached the 10-candidate transition threshold. Shadow-to-primary transition now eligible pending operator approval.

gaps.description: Discovery-intake-queue contract promises a validation checker but none exists in Mission Control scripts
gaps.current_state: No checker script validates queue entries against schema or checks markdown linkage
gaps.desired_state: Checker that validates queue entries against schema, checks markdown forward/backward linkage, and reports sync gaps
gaps.resolution_notes: Discovery queue writes and transitions are now repo-native through shared/lib/discovery-intake-queue-writer.ts and shared/lib/discovery-intake-queue-transition.ts. The older Mission Control checker reference is retired; active queue truth is carried by the canonical queue artifact plus repo-native composition/read surfaces.

gaps.description: Architecture-to-Runtime handoff contract has never been exercised in practice
gaps.current_state: Contract exists at shared/contracts/architecture-to-runtime.md but no record shows it being used
gaps.desired_state: At least one Architecture candidate handed off to Runtime through the contract
gaps.resolution_notes: Formal handoff record created at runtime/legacy-handoff/2026-03-22-autoresearch-architecture-to-runtime-handoff.md. Exercises all required contract fields. Real work (Architecture extraction → Runtime bounded run → promotion) completed 2026-03-19–2026-03-20.

gaps.description: Evaluator contract exists but no candidate has used a real dimensional evaluator with numeric before/after comparison
gaps.current_state: Pilot transformation used pass/fail checks (runtime-sync, typecheck, build), not dimensional measurement
gaps.desired_state: At least one candidate uses a dimensional evaluator (latency, cost, memory, or throughput with numeric baseline and result)
gaps.resolution_notes: Backend test boilerplate consolidation uses dimensional evaluator with numeric before/after: 1,279→1,016 lines (-20.6%), 47,603→39,572 bytes (-16.9%). Both exceed 10% threshold. First non-vacuous dimensional evaluator proof.

gaps.description: Discovery-first intake coverage still trails routing history; the front door needs more native upstream usage and less historical backfill
gaps.current_state: Discovery queue is in primary mode with explicit intake linkage for split-case entries, and executable coverage analysis now measures routine usage from the live corpus instead of hand-counted markdown totals.
gaps.desired_state: All new candidates enter through Discovery queue plus markdown intake; intake coverage grows over time instead of relying on backfill
gaps.resolution_notes: The canonical front-door coverage analysis now lives at shared/lib/discovery-front-door-coverage.ts and measures routine usage from the live corpus. Current metrics: queue in primary mode, 22 total entries, 14 post-primary entries, 13 native-like post-primary entries, and 100% native-like post-primary intake linkage coverage. The lone post-primary missing intake link is a backfill candidate (`al-parked-cli-anything`), so Discovery-first routine usage is confirmed for native work.

gaps.description: Root-level external tool bridge behavior still preserves agent-lab-era assumptions outside the Directive Workspace ownership model
gaps.related_mission_objective: Replaceable integrations and clean product ownership
gaps.current_state: OpenClaw-owned external-tool bridge now resolves through root adapters, current Runtime source packs, and Mission Control runtime dependencies where applicable
gaps.desired_state: No live runtime path depends on workspace/agent-lab internals; remaining historical references are archival only
gaps.resolution_notes: Root runner migrated to scripts/invoke-external-tool.ps1 + scripts/external-tools/*; root writeback helper restored; live adapter paths no longer point to workspace/agent-lab/orchestration

gaps.description: Discovery intake queue has limited primary-mode operational history; needs 5+ native entries to confirm stability
gaps.current_state: 14 entries (5 native, 9 backfill); threshold reached
gaps.desired_state: 5+ candidates processed natively through primary-mode queue
gaps.resolution_notes: 5 native primary-mode queue entries achieved. Three BPT candidates processed end-to-end through native queue.
